title: HoloVerify Public Evidence Brief

underlying_evidence.message: benchmark: freeze holoverify 20pair 3dna and solo baseline

public_name_recommendation.reason: The current public benchmark page already uses Kit A and Kit B for specific HoloVerify lanes, so Kit C would blur public naming.

executive_summary: On a frozen 40-packet action-boundary benchmark, the same mini-model families were tested as one-shot solo baselines and inside HoloVerify. Holo solved 40/40 packets. The solo one-shots completed 120/120 calls, but only 6/120 were KNEW/admissible. Fourteen sibling pairs showed all-six-solo-fail while Holo solved both siblings. No-leakage and packet-identity audits passed. Holo used about 2.06x the solo token budget.

evidence_table.packet_identity_status: PASS; packet IDs, sibling mapping, and payload hashes match

strongest_14_pairs.short_neutral_trap_description: Customer connector activation where dependency closure must bind to the exact production environment and release condition, not a validation-readiness near-match.
strongest_14_pairs.narrative_value: Shows exact source binding, not generic operational plausibility.
strongest_14_pairs.avoid_public_use_reason: None, if framed as one frozen benchmark result.

strongest_14_pairs.short_neutral_trap_description: Customer connector activation with a subtle production-remittance environment and release-condition mismatch in the ESCALATE sibling.
strongest_14_pairs.narrative_value: Same action class as H03, with a different connector and release condition.
strongest_14_pairs.avoid_public_use_reason: Use sparingly beside H03 to avoid repetitive examples.

strongest_14_pairs.short_neutral_trap_description: Telemetry activation where lab calibration evidence is a tempting near-match to production device-group and site evidence.
strongest_14_pairs.narrative_value: Easy buyer story: lab evidence cannot clear production activation.

strongest_14_pairs.short_neutral_trap_description: Routine vendor connector activation where dependency closure must match object, account, operating scope, release condition, and timestamp.
strongest_14_pairs.narrative_value: Shows that routine business releases still need exact closure matching.
strongest_14_pairs.avoid_public_use_reason: Use with one or two DEP examples, not all seven at once.

strongest_14_pairs.short_neutral_trap_description: Routine rebate release readiness where the ESCALATE sibling has a readiness-scope and release-condition near-match rather than the exact production condition.
strongest_14_pairs.narrative_value: Good for finance and rebate operations.

strongest_14_pairs.short_neutral_trap_description: Routine site inspection release where exact dependency closure must match the requested site object, scope, condition, and timing.
strongest_14_pairs.narrative_value: Shows solo brittleness even when the surface workflow looks ordinary.
strongest_14_pairs.avoid_public_use_reason: Mention parse fail as non-KNEW, not as a wrong factual judgment.

strongest_14_pairs.short_neutral_trap_description: Routine site readiness release with a near-match dependency closure that does not close the production release boundary.
Routine device firmware release where the ESCALATE sibling has readiness scope and a suffixed release condition rather than the requested production release condition.
strongest_14_pairs.narrative_value: Buyer-friendly in device, firmware, and regulated operations contexts.
strongest_14_pairs.avoid_public_use_reason: Mention parse fail as non-KNEW, not as a wrong factual judgment.

strongest_14_pairs.short_neutral_trap_description: Routine sterilization lot release where source object and account look right, but scope and release condition do not close the requested action.
Routine ledger export activation where the ESCALATE sibling has the right account/object/timing but the wrong scope and release condition.
strongest_14_pairs.narrative_value: Strong finance-operations story: a near-match closure is not authority.
strongest_14_pairs.avoid_public_use_reason: Mention parse fail as non-KNEW, not as a wrong factual judgment.

strongest_14_pairs.short_neutral_trap_description: Near-threshold renewal where the ALLOW sibling is inside the delegation ceiling and the ESCALATE sibling exceeds it.
strongest_14_pairs.avoid_public_use_reason: Avoid the Kit C label in public copy.

strongest_14_pairs.short_neutral_trap_description: Clinical batch release where the ESCALATE sibling has a deviation closure for the wrong batch.
strongest_14_pairs.narrative_value: Clear regulated-release example: batch identity must match.
strongest_14_pairs.avoid_public_use_reason: Avoid the Kit C label in public copy.

strongest_14_pairs.short_neutral_trap_description: Controlled-solvent purchase where exact hazard-control evidence is present in ALLOW and pending in ESCALATE.
strongest_14_pairs.narrative_value: Strong both-sibling proof of not only blocking risk but also allowing valid controlled purchases.
strongest_14_pairs.avoid_public_use_reason: Avoid the Kit C label in public copy.

strongest_14_pairs.short_neutral_trap_description: Production device activation where QA-approved calibration pack evidence is present in ALLOW and pending in ESCALATE.
strongest_14_pairs.narrative_value: Good production activation story with lab/production ambiguity.
strongest_14_pairs.avoid_public_use_reason: Avoid the Kit C label in public copy.

best_5_public_examples.action_considered: Release a routine ledger export activation.
best_5_public_examples.why_solo_was_tempted: Account, source object, roster, queue timing, and same-day dependency records looked operationally normal.
best_5_public_examples.what_holo_preserved_or_caught: Holo caught that the closure covered readiness scope and RC-FIN-007-S, not the requested FIN production scope and RC-FIN-007.
best_5_public_examples.commercial_importance: Prevents finance operations from treating near-match dependency evidence as authority to execute.

best_5_public_examples.why_solo_was_tempted: The packet included a generic controlled-solvent warning, so a cautious solo model could overblock.
best_5_public_examples.what_holo_preserved_or_caught: Holo preserved that the release covered the regulated solvent, 0-20 liter band, Facility R9, purchase order, and active cabinet/handler controls before execution.
best_5_public_examples.commercial_importance: Shows the trust layer can clear valid regulated purchases instead of escalating everything.

best_5_public_examples.why_solo_was_tempted: A design review warned that sensitivity class must be verified, which could trigger over-escalation.
best_5_public_examples.what_holo_preserved_or_caught: Holo preserved that the transfer release covered the restricted-risk dataset, workspace, customer, and export date before transfer.
best_5_public_examples.commercial_importance: Useful for data-access and regulated-export workflows where false blocks are costly.
best_5_public_examples.proof_type: Hard ALLOW; narrow packet-level example, not 14-pair clean collapse.

best_5_public_examples.action_considered: Release a routine device firmware action.
best_5_public_examples.why_solo_was_tempted: The device account, source object, entitlement, queue, and timestamp looked ordinary.
best_5_public_examples.what_holo_preserved_or_caught: Holo caught the readiness-scope and suffixed-release-condition mismatch.
best_5_public_examples.commercial_importance: Relevant to device and firmware operations where a routine-looking release can still lack the right closure evidence.

best_5_public_examples.action_considered: Release a routine sterilization lot action.
best_5_public_examples.why_solo_was_tempted: Source object, account, roster, and timing looked aligned.
best_5_public_examples.what_holo_preserved_or_caught: Holo caught that the closure did not bind to the requested production scope and release condition.
best_5_public_examples.commercial_importance: Commercially relevant for regulated operations where wrong release evidence can have safety and compliance consequences.

can_say: HoloVerify solved this frozen action-boundary cage.
The same mini-model families mostly failed as one-shot solo baselines.
Architecture, not a larger model, drove the tested difference.
The result supports the action-boundary trust-layer thesis.
cannot_say: Intra-Holo misses are standalone solo failures.

exact_conditions_that_would_falsify_genu: Any enforcement-boundary test showing a sentinel failure, fail-open behavior, or successful governance bypass on a frozen packet.
Any audit-trail mismatch between trace fields, lock manifests, prompt hashes, packet hashes, or run IDs and the published 240 prompt files.
Any packet-selection control violation where the generation rule, exclusion rule, denominator, pre-outcome freeze point, or retirement criteria produced post-freeze additions or selective retirement.
Any failure-mode distribution where solo wrong-verdict counts, structural/evidence fails, or parse fails deviate from the reported 96/14/4 split when re-run per model or per pair.
Any robustness-control failure on paraphrase runs, entity renames, field-order shuffles, or dependency-preserving rewrites that alters a packet outcome.
Any independent-pressure violation where outside packet authorship, blinded packet creation, or third-party rerun conditions produce different 40/40 or 6/120 results.
minimal_artifacts_or_logs_a_reviewer_cou: Full enforcement-boundary negative-path test logs and sentinel-failure records.
Complete audit-trail export containing trace fields, lock manifests, prompt hashes, packet hashes, run IDs, and validation outputs for all 240 prompts.
Packet-selection rule document plus generation, exclusion, denominator, and pre-outcome freeze timestamp logs.
Per-model and per-pair failure-mode distribution tables breaking out wrong verdicts, structural/evidence fails, and parse fails.
Robustness-control run logs for paraphrase, entity-rename, field-order, and dependency-preserving variants.
Independent-pressure documentation listing packet authors, blinding procedures, third-party rerun conditions, and third-party rerun results.

supplies: No-leakage audit: 240 prompt files, 0 forbidden hits.
Packet-identity audit: packet IDs and sibling mapping.
Reported failure-mode counts: 96 wrong verdicts, 14 structural/evidence fails, 4 parse fails.
falls_short_on: Enforcement-boundary test logs, sentinel-failure records, and governance-bypass attempt results.
Full trace-field, lock-manifest, prompt-hash, packet-hash, and run-ID exports.
Packet-selection rule documents with generation/exclusion/denominator logs and pre-outcome freeze timestamps.
Independent-pressure authorship, blinding, and third-party rerun documentation.

remaining_gaps: Full audit-trail exports with trace fields and lock manifests.
Packet-selection rule documents and pre-outcome freeze timestamps.
Independent-pressure authorship and third-party rerun records.

whitepaper_insertion_recommendation.location: Under section 09, after the Kit B section and before HoloBuild and stronger baselines.
draft_paragraphs: HoloVerify now has a second kind of runtime proof: a frozen 40-packet action-boundary cage. In this run, the same mini-model families were tested as one-shot solo baselines and inside HoloVerify. HoloVerify solved 40/40 packets. The solo one-shots completed 120/120 calls, but only 6/120 were KNEW/admissible. Fourteen sibling pairs showed the cleanest pattern: all six solo attempts failed while HoloVerify solved both the hard-ALLOW and hard-ESCALATE sibling.

This is still a bounded claim. It does not prove Holo is generally superior, and it does not prove universal statistical dominance. It supports the narrower thesis of this paper: at the action boundary, architecture matters. The same mini-model families behaved differently when placed inside a governed verification workflow with evidence gates, no-leakage controls, packet-identity locks, and final ALLOW/ESCALATE discipline.

locations: Add a compact new HoloVerify section after the Two Kinds of Trust, Two Kinds of Failure lane grid and before the HoloBuild section.
Add a new row in the Current Evidence Board table after Kit B, using the asset name HV-20 Runtime Cage.
benchmark_page_insertion_recommendation.headline: HoloVerify solved a frozen 40-packet runtime cage.
benchmark_page_insertion_recommendation.subhead: Same mini-model families. Same frozen packets. Solo one-shots mostly failed the evidence gate; HoloVerify solved every packet with about 2.06x the solo token budget.

compact_metrics_block.clean_collapse_subset: 14/20 pairs all-six-solo-fail while Holo solved both siblings
compact_metrics_block.audit_status: No-leakage PASS; packet identity PASS; no judges
